reason: Both answers reach the same recommendation (take it, conditional on a clear plan to deploy capital for acceleration) and correctly cover the default-alive leverage framing, ~17% dilution math, 8%/mo trajectory, and venture-treadmill tradeoff vs. a bootstrapped lifestyle business. Both note negotiating leverage from the term sheet. B is slightly more specific on deal terms (1x non-participating, board control) and offers a concrete negotiation lever, while A has a slightly cleaner decision test. The differences are minor and roughly offset.
